heading: Exceptions to limitations on expenditures
label: 3505 Exceptions to limitations on expenditures

Expansion of Highways in Michigan, , , exempted existing highways in Michigan from limitations on the use of Federal expenditures or financial assistance within the Coastal Barrier Resources System under if the Congress added new units to the Coastal Barrier Resources System under , and those units included portions of United States or State highways in the State of Michigan, prior to repeal by , , . Expansion of Highways in Michigan, , , exempted existing highways in Michigan from limitations on the use of Federal expenditures or financial assistance within the Coastal Barrier Resources System under if the Congress added new units to the Coastal Barrier Resources System under , and those units included portions of United States or State highways in the State of Michigan, prior to repeal by , , . See .Pub. L. 100–707, title II, § 204(d)102 Stat. 471516 U.S.C. 3505(a)(3)16 U.S.C. 3503Pub. L. 101–591, § 5(b)104 Stat. 2936section 3505(c) of this titleNov. 23, 1988Nov. 16, 1990

children.text: Any use or facility necessary for the exploration, extraction, or transportation of energy resources which can be carried out only on, in, or adjacent to a coastal water area because the use or facility requires access to the coastal water body.

The maintenance or construction of improvements of existing Federal navigation channels (including the Intracoastal Waterway) and related structures (such as jetties), including the disposal of dredge materials related to such maintenance or construction.

The maintenance, replacement, reconstruction, or repair, but not the expansion, of publicly owned or publicly operated roads, structures, or facilities that are essential links in a larger network or system.
Military activities essential to national security.
The construction, operation, maintenance, and rehabilitation of Coast Guard facilities and access thereto.

children.label: A
children.text: Projects for the study, management, protection, and enhancement of fish and wildlife resources and habitats, including acquisition of fish and wildlife habitats and related lands, stabilization projects for fish and wildlife habitats, and recreational projects.

children.label: B
children.text: Establishment, operation, and maintenance of air and water navigation aids and devices, and for access thereto.

children.label: C
children.text: Projects under chapter 2003 of title 54 and the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1451

children.label: D
children.text: Scientific research, including aeronautical, atmospheric, space, geologic, marine, fish and wildlife, and other research, development, and applications.

children.label: E
children.text: Emergency actions necessary to the saving of lives and the protection of property and the public health and safety, if such actions are performed pursuant to sections 5170a, 5170b, 5173, and 5192 of title 42 and are limited to actions that are necessary to alleviate the applicable emergency.

children.label: F
children.text: Maintenance, replacement, reconstruction, or repair, but not the expansion (except with respect to United States route 1 in the Florida Keys), of publicly owned or publicly operated roads, structures, and facilities.

children.label: G
children.text: Nonstructural projects for shoreline stabilization that are designed to mimic, enhance, or restore a natural stabilization system.

children.label: i
children.text: produce shellfish (including oysters, clams, and mussels), cultivate micro- or macro-algae, or do not require the use of aquaculture feeds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: adhere to best management practices and conservation measures recommended by the Secretary through the consultation process referred to in this subsection.

Use of a sand source within a System unit by Federal coastal storm risk management projects or their predecessor projects that have used a System unit for sand to nourish adjacent beaches outside the System pursuant to at least once between and in response to an emergency situation prior to .section 701n of title 33December 31, 2008December 31, 2023December 31, 2023

children.heading: Existing Federal navigation channels
children.label: b Existing Federal navigation channels
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(2), a Federal navigation channel or a related structure is an existing channel or structure, respectively, if it was authorized before the date on which the relevant System unit or portion of the System unit was included within the System.

children.heading: Expansion of highways in Michigan
children.label: c Expansion of highways in Michigan

children.text: located in a unit of the System in Michigan; and
in existence on .November 16, 1990

children.heading: Services and facilities outside System
children.label: d Services and facilities outside System

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3) of this subsection, limitations on the use of Federal expenditures or financial assistance within the System under shall not apply to expenditures or assistance provided for services or facilities and related infrastructure located outside the boundaries of unit T–11 of the System (as depicted on the maps referred to in ) which relate to an activity within that unit.section 3504 of this titlesection 3503(a) of this title

children.heading: Prohibition of flood insurance coverage
children.label: 2 Prohibition of flood insurance coverage
children.text: No new flood insurance coverage may be provided under the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 ( et seq.) for any new construction or substantial improvements relating to services or facilities and related infrastructure located outside the boundaries of unit T–11 of the System that facilitate an activity within that unit that is not consistent with the purposes of this chapter.42 U.S.C. 4001

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No financial assistance for acquisition, construction, or improvement purposes may be provided under any program administered by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development for any services or facilities and related infrastructure located outside the boundaries of unit T–11 of the System that facilitate an activity within that unit that is not consistent with the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: “Financial assistance” defined
children.label: B “Financial assistance” defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “financial assistance” includes any contract, loan, grant, cooperative agreement, or other form of assistance, including the insurance or guarantee of a loan, mortgage, or pool of mortgages.

children.heading: Applicability to Otherwise Protected Areas
children.label: e Applicability to Otherwise Protected Areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The prohibitions on new Federal expenditures and financial assistance described in do not apply within Otherwise Protected Areas except with respect to limitations on new flood insurance coverage described in section 1321 of the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 ().section 3504(a) of this title42 U.S.C. 4028

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), new Federal flood insurance may be provided for a structure in an Otherwise Protected Area that is used in a manner consistent with the purpose for which such Otherwise Protected Area is protected.

children.heading: Applicability to additions and insurable structures
children.label: f Applicability to additions and insurable structures

children.heading: Additions
children.label: 1 Additions
children.text: With respect to an addition to the System made under on or after , subject to paragraph (2), the prohibitions on new Federal expenditures and financial assistance described in shall take effect on the date that is 1 year after the date on which such addition is made.section 3503 of this titlesection 3504(a) of this titleNovember 25, 2024

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The prohibitions on new Federal expenditures and financial assistance described in do not apply to an insurable structure.section 3504(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: located within an addition described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: in existence before the expiration of the applicable 1-year period described in paragraph (1).
